accuracy.description: Fraction of predictions that exactly match the true labels.

accuracy_parity_difference.description: Gap in per-group accuracy between the best- and worst-served groups.

aliases: does my es estimate hold up
acerbi_szekely_z1.description: Tests whether realized losses match the model's expected shortfall, conditional on VaR breaches.

acerbi_szekely_z2.description: Tests expected-shortfall calibration across the full sample without conditioning on breaches.

aliases: beating the benchmark by how much
active_return.description: Annualized average return earned above the benchmark.

active_share.description: Fraction of a portfolio that differs from its benchmark holdings.

aliases: how well the model fits after penalizing for extra inputs
the goodness of fit that does not reward adding more variables
fit quality once you account for how many factors you used
explained variation corrected for model bloat
how much the data is explained without crediting useless predictors
fit score that discounts throwing in more columns
adjusted_r2.description: R-squared penalized for the number of predictors in the model.

adjusted_rand_index.description: Chance-corrected agreement between two clusterings, scaled so random equals zero.

aliases: sharpe corrected for skew and fat tails
adjusted_sharpe_ratio.description: Sharpe ratio penalized for negative skew and excess kurtosis in returns.

aic.description: Model fit score trading likelihood against parameter count, lower is better.

aicc.description: Small-sample corrected Akaike criterion, lower indicates a better model.

alpha.description: Return earned beyond what the benchmark exposure (beta) would predict.

alpha_tstat.description: T-statistic on alpha measuring whether outperformance is statistically significant.

altman_z.description: Bankruptcy-risk score combining five financial ratios for public manufacturers.

altman_z_prime.description: Altman bankruptcy score variant for private firms using four ratios.

amihud_illiquidity.description: Average ratio of absolute return to dollar volume, measuring price impact per traded dollar.

amivest_liquidity.description: Average ratio of dollar volume to absolute return, measuring liquidity per unit of price move.

anderson_darling.description: Tests whether a sample comes from a specified distribution, weighting the tails heavily.

annuity_factor.description: Present value of one unit paid each period over a discounted horizon.

anova.description: Tests whether the means of three or more groups differ significantly.

ansari_bradley.description: Nonparametric test comparing the spread or scale of two samples.

apdex.description: User satisfaction score from response times against a target threshold.

aliases: alpha per unit of residual risk
appraisal_ratio.description: Alpha divided by idiosyncratic risk, measuring risk-adjusted stock-picking skill.

arch_lm.description: Tests for autoregressive conditional heteroskedasticity (volatility clustering) in a series.

arhr_at_k.description: Average reciprocal hit rank over the top-k results across queries.

arrival_slippage.description: Cost of execution measured against the price when the order arrived.

asrf_capital_requirement.description: Basel regulatory capital per unit exposure under the single-factor credit model.

asrf_rwa.description: Risk-weighted assets from the Basel single-factor credit capital model.

asset_turnover.description: How much revenue each dollar of assets generates.

atkinson_index.description: Inequality measure capturing welfare loss from unequal distribution, tunable by aversion parameter.

auc.description: Probability a random positive scores higher than a random negative.

autocorrelation.description: Measures linear correlation of a series with its own lagged values.

availability.description: Fraction of time a system was up versus total time.

average_treatment_effect.description: Mean difference in outcome between treated and control units.

balanced_accuracy.description: Average of per-class recall, robust to class imbalance.

bartlett.description: Tests whether several groups have equal variances, assuming normality.

basel_traffic_light.description: Regulatory zone (green/yellow/red) grading VaR model accuracy by breach count.

aliases: percent of periods beating the index
batting_average.description: Fraction of periods the portfolio beat its benchmark.

bcubed_f1.description: Harmonic mean of B-cubed precision and recall for clustering quality.

bcubed_precision.description: Average fraction of an item's cluster sharing its true class.

bcubed_recall.description: Average fraction of an item's true class landing in its cluster.

aliases: how much we drop when market drops
bear_beta.description: Portfolio's sensitivity to the benchmark during down markets only.

benjamini_yekutieli.description: Controls false discovery rate across many tests under arbitrary dependence.

berger_parker.description: Dominance index equal to the proportional abundance of the single most common category.

aliases: how much it moves with the market
beta.description: Sensitivity of returns to benchmark moves; market exposure.

beta_tstat.description: T-statistic on beta measuring whether market sensitivity is statistically significant.

aliases: how many findings survive after correcting for testing lots of things at once
count of results that still hold up once you account for many comparisons
which of my many tests are real and not just luck from running so many
filtering out false alarms when you check hundreds of things
number of true hits after controlling the rate of false discoveries
how many signals remain once i adjust for fishing across many tests
weeding out the flukes when running a big batch of tests
bh_rejections.description: Counts hypotheses rejected by the Benjamini-Hochberg false-discovery-rate procedure.

bhattacharyya_distance.description: Measures dissimilarity between two probability distributions via their overlap.

bias_ratio.description: Detects return smoothing or pricing manipulation by comparing returns just above and below zero.

bic.description: Model selection score penalizing parameters more strongly with sample size.

bipower_variation.description: Estimates continuous (jump-free) variance from products of adjacent absolute returns.

aliases: what a loan-like investment is worth today given its future payouts
the fair value of a debt note based on its interest and payback
discounting all the coupon payments and the final repayment back to now
how much you'd pay upfront for a stream of fixed interest plus principal
present-day worth of a fixed-income holding
value of an iou after accounting for the going rate of return
what the market thinks a borrowing certificate is currently worth
bond_price.description: Present value of a bond's cash flows discounted to today.

bonferroni_rejections.description: Counts hypotheses rejected after the conservative Bonferroni multiple-comparison correction.

bowley_skewness.description: Quartile-based robust measure of distribution asymmetry.

box_pierce.description: Tests whether a group of autocorrelations is jointly zero (white noise).

brennan_prediger.description: Chance-corrected inter-rater agreement assuming uniform expected agreement.

brier.description: Mean squared error between predicted probabilities and outcomes.

brier_reliability.description: Calibration component of the Brier score measuring predicted-vs-observed gap.

aliases: how much the predicted chances actually spread the outcomes apart
do its confidence levels separate the wins from the losses
how well its guesses tell different cases apart
the part of the score that rewards telling groups apart
does it give higher odds when things really do happen more often
how much its probability calls vary with what actually occurs
credit for sorting outcomes into meaningfully different buckets
brier_resolution.description: Discrimination component of the Brier score rewarding outcome-separating forecasts.

brier_skill_score.description: Brier score improvement over a baseline forecast, higher is better.

brinson_allocation.description: Portion of active return from over- or under-weighting sectors versus the benchmark.

aliases: how much of the outperformance came from picking the right sectors to overweight
the part of beating the index explained by where you put your money, not what you bought
value added by leaning into the right buckets versus the benchmark
gain from betting more on areas that did better than the overall market
how your weighting choices across groups helped beat the yardstick
the contribution of being heavy or light in segments relative to the index
credit for choosing winning categories rather than winning individual names
brinson_fachler_allocation.description: Allocation effect measured relative to total benchmark return, per Brinson-Fachler.

aliases: the bonus from both picking good stocks and overweighting them at the same time
the combined effect of choosing the right group and the right names within it
leftover performance that comes from selection and weighting working together
the cross term where good picks and big bets reinforce each other
extra return when you put more money exactly where your stock picks paid off
the overlap piece that isn't pure sector tilt or pure name selection
how weighting and stock choice multiply rather than just add up
brinson_interaction.description: Active return from the combined effect of weighting and selection decisions.

brinson_selection.description: Active return from picking better securities within each benchmark sector.

brinson_total_active.description: Total active return decomposed across allocation, selection, and interaction effects.

brunner_munzel.description: Nonparametric test of stochastic equality between two samples without equal-variance assumption.

bs_charm.description: Rate of change of option delta as time passes (delta decay).

bs_color.description: Rate of change of option gamma with respect to passage of time.

bs_delta.description: Sensitivity of option price to a change in the underlying spot.

aliases: how fast my option's directional sensitivity changes
rate of change of the hedge ratio as the stock moves
curvature of the option price versus the underlying
how quickly my delta shifts when the price moves
how much my exposure accelerates as the stock moves
the bend in how option value tracks the underlying
how unstable my directional bet gets with price moves
bs_gamma.description: Rate of change of option delta with respect to the underlying spot.

bs_implied_vol.description: Volatility that makes the Black-Scholes price match the observed option price.

bs_lambda.description: Percentage change in option price for a percentage change in the underlying (elasticity).

bs_rho.description: Sensitivity of option price to a change in the risk-free interest rate.

bs_speed.description: Rate of change of option gamma with respect to the underlying spot.

aliases: how much value the option loses each day just from time passing
the daily bleed on my option from getting closer to expiry
how fast my option decays as time runs out
cost of holding the option as another day goes by
time decay eating into the option's worth
how much the clock alone drains the option price
daily erosion of the option value with nothing else changing
bs_theta.description: Sensitivity of option price to the passage of time (time decay).

bs_ultima.description: Third-order sensitivity of option price to changes in volatility.

bs_value.description: Black-Scholes fair value (theoretical price) of a European option.

bs_vanna.description: Sensitivity of option delta to volatility, or vega to spot.

bs_vega.description: Sensitivity of option price to a change in volatility.

bs_veta.description: Rate of change of option vega with respect to passage of time.

bs_volga.description: Sensitivity of option vega to a change in volatility (vol convexity).

bs_zomma.description: Rate of change of option gamma with respect to volatility.

aliases: how much we gain when market rises
bull_beta.description: Portfolio's sensitivity to the benchmark during up markets only.

aliases: return per square root of drawdowns
burke_ratio.description: Excess return divided by the root-sum-square of drawdowns.

cache_hit_rate.description: Fraction of requests served from cache rather than recomputed.

cagr.description: Smoothed annual growth rate of a value over multiple years.

calibration_in_the_large.description: Overall difference between mean predicted probability and observed event rate.

called_pct.description: Share of committed capital that an investor has actually contributed.

calmar.description: Annualized return divided by maximum drawdown.

capture_ratio.description: Up-capture divided by down-capture; asymmetry of participation in market moves.

carried_interest.description: Fund manager's profit share earned above returning investor capital.

cdar.description: Average of the worst drawdowns beyond a chosen confidence level.

chi_square.description: Tests association between two categorical variables from a contingency table.

chi_square_distance.description: Measures dissimilarity between two distributions using a chi-square-weighted difference.

christoffersen_cc.description: Joint test of correct VaR breach frequency and breach independence over time.

christoffersen_independence.description: Tests whether VaR breaches cluster in time rather than occurring independently.

churn_rate.description: Fraction of customers lost over a period.

cliffs_delta.description: Ordinal effect size for how often one group exceeds another.

coefficient_of_variation.description: Ratio of standard deviation to mean, expressing relative variability.

cohen_kappa.description: Inter-rater agreement corrected for chance agreement.

cohens_h.description: Effect size for the difference between two proportions using arcsine transform.

column_max.description: Largest value in a numeric column.

column_mean.description: Arithmetic average of the values in a column.

column_median.description: Middle value of a column when sorted, the 50th percentile.

column_min.description: Smallest value in a numeric column.

column_std.description: Sample standard deviation measuring spread around the column mean.

column_sum.description: Total of all values added together in a column.

common_language_effect_size.description: Probability a random value from one group exceeds one from another.

common_sense_ratio.description: Tail ratio multiplied by profit factor; combines tail asymmetry and gain/loss balance.

completeness_score.description: Whether all members of a true class fall in one cluster.

comprehensive_concentration_index.description: Market concentration index weighting each firm's share by its relative rank importance.

aliases: how much smaller the file got after squeezing it
the shrink factor compared to the original size
how many times smaller it is now
space saved when packing the data down
original size divided by the squeezed size
how tightly the data was packed
before size versus after size for storage
compression_ratio.description: Ratio of original size to compressed size of the data.

aliases: how much the top few control
share held by the biggest players
do a handful dominate the market
slice owned by the largest few combined
how clustered around the leaders it is
fraction taken by the top performers
are the big ones running the whole thing
concentration_ratio.description: Combined market share held by the largest few firms or categories.

concordance_correlation.description: Agreement between predictions and targets combining correlation and bias.

concordance_index.description: Fraction of comparable pairs whose predicted risk ordering matches observed survival.

confidence_interval.description: Range plausibly containing the true mean at a given confidence level.

convexity.description: Curvature of a bond's price response to interest-rate changes.

cornish_fisher_var.description: Value-at-risk adjusted for skew and kurtosis via the Cornish-Fisher expansion.

correlation.description: Strength and direction of the linear or monotonic relationship between two variables.

covariance.description: Measure of how two variables move together, jointly above or below their means.

coverage_deviation.description: Gap between empirical interval coverage and the nominal target level.

cramers_v.description: Effect size for association strength between two categorical variables.

cumulative_forecast_error.description: Running sum of forecast errors over the horizon.

cuped_ate.description: Variance-reduced treatment effect using a pre-experiment covariate adjustment.

current_ratio.description: Short-term liquidity from current assets divided by current liabilities.

curve_pv.description: Present value of cash flows discounted along a zero-rate curve.

cvar.description: Average loss in the worst tail beyond the VaR threshold.

aliases: how much better the model is than just guessing the middle value
the share of typical error the model wipes out
fraction of average miss explained away by the predictions
how much of the spread in errors the model accounts for
improvement over a naive median guess
portion of the absolute error the model gets rid of
d2_absolute_error.description: Fraction of absolute error explained relative to a median baseline.

d2_tweedie_score.description: Fraction of Tweedie deviance explained relative to a mean baseline.

days_sales_outstanding.description: Average days to collect cash from credit sales.

debt_to_ebitda.description: Leverage measured as total debt over operating earnings.

debt_to_equity.description: Leverage comparing total debt to shareholder equity.

demographic_parity_difference.description: Difference in positive-prediction rate across protected groups.

demographic_parity_ratio.description: Ratio of positive-prediction rates between protected groups.

aliases: how much better the odds tilt right when the test fires versus when it doesn't
one number for how good the whole yes-no test is
the odds of a real case testing positive versus a clear case testing positive
overall strength of a flag at separating sick from healthy
how lopsided the right-versus-wrong calls are between positives and negatives
a single ratio summing up how trustworthy the test is both ways
diagnostic_odds_ratio.description: Ratio of positive to negative likelihood ratios summarizing test discrimination.

direct_alpha.description: Annualized excess return of a fund versus a public-market index.

distance_correlation.description: Measures both linear and nonlinear dependence between two variables.

distinct_count.description: Number of unique values present in a column.

dividend_payout_ratio.description: Portion of net income paid out to shareholders as dividends.

aliases: how much we lose in down markets
down_capture_ratio.description: Portfolio return relative to benchmark during the benchmark's down periods.

downside_deviation.description: Volatility computed only from returns below a target, ignoring upside.

downside_potential.description: Average shortfall of returns below a target threshold.

dpi.description: Cash returned to investors per dollar of contributed capital.

drawdown_at_risk.description: Drawdown quantile at a confidence level; threshold the drawdown rarely exceeds.

drawdown_deviation.description: Standard deviation of the drawdown series.

duplicate_count.description: Number of rows that repeat an earlier identical row.

durbin_watson.description: Tests residuals for autocorrelation, with 2 meaning no correlation.

aliases: price value of a basis point
dv01.description: Dollar price change of a bond per one basis-point rate move.

ebitda_margin.description: Operating profitability as EBITDA divided by revenue.

ece.description: Average binned gap between predicted confidence and actual accuracy.

effect_size.description: Standardized magnitude of the difference between two group means.

effective_convexity.description: Convexity estimated by shifting rates, accounting for embedded options.

effective_duration.description: Rate sensitivity estimated by shifting yields, capturing optionality.

effective_number_of_bets.description: Diversification measure counting how many independent positions a portfolio effectively holds.

effective_spread_bps.description: Twice the signed distance between execution price and the prevailing midpoint, in basis points.

energy_distance.description: Statistical distance between two distributions based on pairwise distances.

entropy.description: Shannon entropy quantifying uncertainty or diversity across category frequencies.

equal_opportunity_difference.description: Difference in true-positive rate across protected groups.

equalized_odds_difference.description: Largest gap in true- or false-positive rates across groups.

equalized_odds_ratio.description: Smallest ratio of true- or false-positive rates across groups.

err_at_k.description: Expected reciprocal rank reflecting graded relevance and user-stopping behavior over top-k.

aliases: how fast we are using up our allowed failures
the rate we are eating into our slack for outages
how quickly the room for mistakes is running out
are we spending our downtime allowance too fast
pace at which we approach the breaking limit
how much of our wiggle room is gone
speed of chewing through the failure allowance
error_budget_burn.description: Rate at which an SLO error budget is being consumed.

error_rate.description: Fraction of requests that failed out of total requests.

es_backtest_ratio.description: Ratio of realized tail losses to predicted expected shortfall during breaches.

aliases: how much of the spread is explained by which group something belongs to
share of the variation that the grouping accounts for
how strongly the category drives the differences i see
portion of the ups and downs explained by the group split
how big a chunk of the variability comes from the groups
strength of the group effect on the outcome
what fraction of the differences the labels explain
eta_squared.description: Proportion of total variance explained by group membership in ANOVA.

aliases: did the answer come out word for word the same
how often the response is exactly the expected text
share of replies that match the gold answer perfectly
did it nail the answer letter for letter after tidying up spacing
right only if the whole thing matches, nothing close counts
how often output equals the reference string outright
exact_match.description: Whether the prediction exactly equals the reference after normalization.

aliases: how far past var losses go
expected_exceedance.description: Average amount by which losses exceed VaR when a breach occurs.

expected_loss.description: Average credit loss as probability of default times loss times exposure.

expected_loss_rate.description: Expected credit loss expressed as a fraction of exposure.

explained_variance.description: Fraction of target variance captured by the model predictions.

exponential_loss.description: Exponentially weighted margin loss used by boosting algorithms.

exposure_weighted_maturity.description: Average maturity of credits weighted by exposure size.

exposure_weighted_pd.description: Average default probability weighted by exposure size.

aliases: harmonic mean of precision and recall
f1.description: Harmonic mean of precision and recall for the positive class.

f1_at_k.description: Harmonic mean of precision and recall over the top-k retrieved results.

fallout_at_k.description: Fraction of irrelevant items retrieved within the top-k (false positive rate).

false_discovery_rate.description: Fraction of positive predictions that are actually wrong.

false_negative_rate.description: Fraction of actual positives the model misses.

false_positive_rate.description: Fraction of actual negatives wrongly predicted positive.

fbeta.description: Weighted harmonic mean of precision and recall tunable by beta.

fisher_exact.description: Exact test of association in a 2x2 table for small samples.

fligner.description: Robust nonparametric test for equality of variances across groups.

forecast_bias.description: Systematic tendency of forecasts to over or under predict.

forward_rate.description: Future interest rate implied between two points on the zero curve.

aliases: geometric mean of precision and recall
fowlkes_mallows.description: Geometric mean of precision and recall from confusion counts.

fowlkes_mallows_clustering.description: Geometric mean of pairwise precision and recall between two clusterings.

fpr_parity_difference.description: Difference in false-positive rate across protected groups.

aliases: the slice of sales that turns into spendable cash after spending on the business
leftover cash from operations as a share of revenue
how much real cash each dollar of sales generates once investments are covered
the portion of top-line money that ends up as truly available cash
cash a company keeps per dollar earned after running and reinvesting
what fraction of revenue becomes cash you could actually pocket
spendable cash generation relative to how much was sold
free_cash_flow_margin.description: Free cash flow as a share of revenue.

g_mean.description: Geometric mean of sensitivity and specificity for imbalanced data.

g_test.description: Likelihood-ratio test of association in a contingency table.

aliases: average up move vs down move
gain_loss_ratio.description: Average gain divided by average loss in magnitude.

gain_to_pain_ratio.description: Sum of returns divided by the absolute sum of losing returns.

gamma_deviance.description: Mean deviance loss assuming a Gamma-distributed target.

garman_klass_volatility.description: Range-based volatility estimator using open, high, low, and close prices.

gastwirth_location.description: Robust location estimator combining weighted central quantiles for resistance to outliers.

aliases: how uneven the spread is overall
tunable measure of how lopsided things are
disparity that you can weight toward the top or bottom
how mixed up versus concentrated the values are
how far from an even split it sits
imbalance that can lean on the rich or the poor end
generalized_entropy_index.description: Family of inequality measures parameterized by sensitivity to different parts of the distribution.

aliases: outperformance computed as a ratio rather than a subtraction
relative gain over the index, geometrically
how much you beat the yardstick when returns are chained together
the multiplicative version of return above the comparison
growth over the benchmark linked across periods instead of differenced
the compounded edge you got versus the reference, not the simple gap
geometric_excess_return.description: Portfolio return relative to benchmark computed geometrically rather than by subtraction.

aliases: typical value when things multiply together
average that works for growth rates
central number for ratios and percentages
the multiplicative middle of the numbers
average that won't be wrecked by one huge value
compounded typical level over the data
geometric_mean.description: Nth root of the product of values, the average for rates and ratios.

geometric_mean_absolute_error.description: Geometric mean of absolute forecast errors, robust to large outliers.

aliases: are a few hogging most of it
fairness of the split between everyone
disparity between the haves and have-nots
how lopsided the pie is sliced
gini_coefficient.description: Inequality measure from 0 (perfect equality) to 1 (maximal concentration).

aliases: average gap between any two values
typical difference when you compare pairs
how far apart two random picks tend to be
how much any two entries usually differ
gini_mean_difference.description: Average absolute difference between all pairs of values, a spread measure.

gini_norm.description: Normalized Gini coefficient equal to twice AUC minus one.

glass_delta.description: Standardized mean difference scaled by the control group's standard deviation.

goodman_kruskal_gamma.description: Ordinal association measure based on concordant minus discordant pairs.

gross_exposure.description: Sum of absolute long and short position values relative to capital.

aliases: roll up the numbers per group
sum or average within each bucket
the figures split out by segment
combine values for each group separately
groupby_aggregate.description: Aggregated value such as sum or mean computed per group.

growth_rate.description: Percentage change in a value from the first period to the last.

gwet_ac1.description: Chance-corrected inter-rater agreement robust to prevalence skew.

harmonic_mean.description: Reciprocal of the average of reciprocals, suited to averaging rates.

hedges_g.description: Bias-corrected standardized mean difference for small samples.

hellinger_distance.description: Bounded distance measuring similarity between two probability distributions.

hhi.description: Herfindahl-Hirschman index summing squared market shares to measure concentration.

hill_estimator.description: Estimates the tail index (heaviness) of a heavy-tailed distribution from extreme values.

aliases: how many kinds there effectively are
the effective variety in the mix
how many distinct types really matter
hill_number.description: Effective number of categories at a chosen diversity order parameter.

hinge_loss.description: Margin-based loss penalizing predictions inside the margin.

hochberg_rejections.description: Counts hypotheses rejected by Hochberg's step-up multiple-comparison procedure.

hodges_lehmann_estimator.description: Robust median of all pairwise averages, resistant to outliers.

holm_rejections.description: Counts hypotheses rejected by Holm's step-down multiple-comparison procedure.

holm_sidak_rejections.description: Counts hypotheses rejected by the Holm-Sidak step-down correction.

homogeneity_score.description: Whether each cluster contains only members of one true class.

hoover_index.description: Share of total that would need redistributing to achieve perfect equality.

hqic.description: Hannan-Quinn model selection score, lower indicates better fit.

huber_loss.description: Error loss that is quadratic for small residuals and linear for large ones.

hurst_rs.description: Hurst exponent via rescaled range; measures long-memory or trending vs mean-reversion.

idiosyncratic_volatility.description: Volatility of returns not explained by the benchmark; firm-specific risk.

implementation_shortfall.description: Total cost between the decision price and actual execution, including unfilled opportunity cost.

index_of_dispersion.description: Variance-to-mean ratio indicating clustering or spread relative to a Poisson baseline.

information_ratio.description: Active return divided by tracking error; consistency of benchmark outperformance.

information_value.description: Predictive strength of a feature for separating a binary outcome.

interest_coverage.description: Ability to pay interest from operating earnings.

interval_coverage.description: Fraction of actual values falling inside the predicted intervals.

iqr.description: Interquartile range, the spread between the 75th and 25th percentiles.

irr.description: Discount rate that makes a cash-flow stream's net present value zero.

jaccard.description: Intersection over union of predicted and true positive sets.

jarque_bera.description: Tests normality using sample skewness and kurtosis jointly.

jeffreys_divergence.description: Symmetric KL-based divergence between two probability distributions.

aliases: how much the timing wobbles from one moment to the next
how uneven the gaps between arrivals are
variation in how long things take to show up
the bounciness of the response timing
spread in the wait times between packets
jitter.description: Variability in latency, typically the spread of response times.

join_row_loss.description: Rows dropped or added when joining tables, detecting unintended row loss or fan-out.

js_divergence.description: Symmetric, bounded measure of similarity between two probability distributions.

jump_variation.description: Variance attributable to price jumps, isolated from continuous diffusion.

kappa_three.description: Return over the cubic root of the third lower partial moment; downside-skew-aware ratio.

kelly_criterion.description: Optimal fraction of capital to bet given win rate and win/loss size.

kendall_tau.description: Rank correlation based on concordant versus discordant pairs.

kl_divergence.description: Asymmetric measure of how one distribution diverges from another.

kling_gupta_efficiency.description: Forecast skill score combining correlation, bias, and variability ratios.

km_median_survival.description: Time at which Kaplan-Meier estimated survival probability reaches one half.

km_survival_at.description: Kaplan-Meier estimated survival probability at a specified time.

kruskal_wallis.description: Nonparametric test of whether three or more groups have equal distributions.

ks_2samp.description: Tests whether two samples come from the same distribution.

ks_pme.description: Kaplan-Schoar public market equivalent comparing fund to index returns.

ks_statistic.description: Maximum separation between positive and negative score distributions.

aliases: do these two sets of numbers come from the same shape of distribution
how different are the overall shapes of two samples
biggest gap between two cumulative distributions
checking whether two datasets follow the same pattern
are these numbers spread out the same way as those
comparing the full shape of one sample against another
whether my data matches an expected distribution shape
ks_test.description: Tests whether two samples share the same underlying distribution.

kupiec_pof.description: Likelihood-ratio test of whether VaR breach frequency matches the expected rate.

kurtosis.description: Measures the heaviness of a distribution's tails relative to normal.

kyle_lambda.description: Price impact coefficient relating price changes to signed order flow.

l_cv.description: L-moment coefficient of variation, a robust relative dispersion measure.

l_kurtosis.description: L-moment based robust measure of distribution tail weight.

l_scale.description: Second L-moment measuring scale, a robust alternative to standard deviation.

l_skewness.description: L-moment based robust measure of distribution asymmetry.

largest_position.description: Size of the single biggest position as a share of the portfolio.

latency_p50.description: Median response time, the 50th percentile of latencies.

the slowest quarter starts above this point

how long a typical-to-slow request takes

all but the worst five percent come in faster
ninety five out of a hundred finished quicker than this
how long most requests take excluding the slow tail

tail response time near the top end

how slow it gets for the unluckiest hundredth
ninety nine out of a hundred finished faster
the far tail of response times
almost everything is quicker than this except the rare slow one
only one in a thousand requests is slower than this
the very worst tenth of a percent starts here
how slow the rarest unlucky calls get
nine hundred ninety nine out of a thousand are faster
the extreme far edge of the slow tail
almost no request is slower than this except one in a thousand

latency_stddev.description: Standard deviation of response times measuring latency spread.

aliases: do the groups have the same amount of spread
checking if the wobble is equal across groups

is the spread consistent across the categories
whether the noise level is equal in each group

aliases: how much better the new version did versus the old one
the boost from switching to the treatment

the bump i got from the new approach

how much higher one group landed compared to the baseline
lift.description: Relative or absolute improvement of one group's mean over another.

liquidity_coverage.description: Share of the portfolio liquidatable within a given number of days.

ljung_box.description: Tests whether a group of autocorrelations is jointly zero.

log_likelihood_gaussian.description: Gaussian log-likelihood of residuals, higher means a better fit.

log_loss.description: Average negative log-likelihood of predicted class probabilities.

long_exposure.description: Total value of long positions relative to capital.

aliases: how big the bets you own are compared to the bets you've sold against
the balance between holdings you expect to rise and ones you expect to fall
size of upside positions relative to downside positions

the tilt between what you're holding and what you've borrowed to sell

long_short_ratio.description: Ratio of long exposure to short exposure in the portfolio.

m2_measure.description: Risk-adjusted return scaled to the benchmark's volatility for direct comparison.

macaulay_duration.description: Cash-flow-weighted average time to receive a bond's payments.

macro_f1.description: Unweighted average of per-class F1 scores.

mae.description: Average absolute difference between predictions and targets.

mann_whitney.description: Nonparametric test of whether one sample tends to exceed another.

map_at_k.description: Mean of average precision over queries, truncated at rank k.

mape.description: Average absolute error expressed as a percentage of actual values.

margin_pct.description: Profit as a percentage of revenue after costs.

markedness.description: Sum of precision and negative predictive value minus one.

martin_ratio.description: Annualized return divided by the ulcer index.

mase.description: Forecast error scaled against a naive seasonal benchmark.

max_consecutive_losses.description: Longest run of consecutive losing periods.

max_drawdown.description: Worst peak-to-trough decline of the equity curve.

aliases: longest time below high water mark
max_drawdown_duration.description: Longest stretch the equity curve stayed below a prior peak.

max_error.description: Largest single absolute residual between prediction and target.

max_to_sum_ratio.description: Largest single value over the total sum; tail dominance diagnostic.

maximum_calibration_error.description: Largest binned gap between predicted confidence and actual accuracy.

mcc.description: Correlation between predicted and true labels from the confusion matrix.

mcnemar.description: Tests for changes in paired binary outcomes between two conditions.

mean_abs_deviation.description: Average absolute distance of values from their mean.

mean_arctangent_ape.description: Arctangent-transformed percentage error, bounded and robust to zeros.

mean_average_precision.description: Mean over queries of the average precision across all relevant items.

mean_directional_accuracy.description: Fraction of times the forecast predicts the correct direction of change.

mean_interval_width.description: Average width of the predicted lower-to-upper intervals.

mean_log_deviation.description: Inequality measure averaging the log gap between mean and individual values.

mean_percentage_error.description: Average signed percentage error, indicating forecast bias direction.

mean_squared_error.description: Average of squared differences between predictions and targets.

mean_tweedie_deviance.description: Average Tweedie deviance loss for the chosen power parameter.

medae.description: Median of absolute residuals, robust to outliers.

median_abs_deviation.description: Median of absolute deviations from the median, a robust spread measure.

median_absolute_percentage_error.description: Median of absolute percentage errors, robust to outlier errors.

merton_distance_to_default.description: Standard deviations of asset value separating a firm from default.

merton_pd.description: Default probability from the Merton structural firm-value model.

micro_f1.description: F1 computed from globally pooled true positives, false positives, and negatives.

midhinge.description: Average of the first and third quartiles, a robust center estimate.

mode_share.description: Fraction of rows held by the single most frequent value.

modified_duration.description: Percentage price change of a bond per unit change in yield.

modified_sharpe_ratio.description: Sharpe-style ratio using modified (Cornish-Fisher) VaR as the risk denominator.

aliases: do two groups have the same amount of variability without assuming a bell curve

comparing dispersion between groups using ranks
whether one group swings more than the other, distribution-free
testing equal spread when the data is messy or skewed
is the variability the same across two sets without normality

moors_kurtosis.description: Octile-based robust measure of distribution tail weight.

mrr.description: Average of the reciprocal rank of the first relevant result per query.

msle.description: Mean squared error computed on log-transformed values.

mtbf.description: Average operating time between consecutive system failures.

aliases: how much knowing one grouping tells you about the other
how much two ways of splitting the data agree by sharing information
do these two labelings move together at all
how much uncertainty about one set of groups drops once you see the other

how predictable one partition is from another
mutual_info_score.description: Shared information between two label assignments measured in nats.

aliases: how much better the forecast is than just using the long run average
whether the predictions beat simply guessing the mean every time
how well the model tracks the ups and downs versus a flat line
fraction of the natural variation the model captures

how closely predictions follow the real swings compared to the average
nash_sutcliffe_efficiency.description: Forecast skill relative to using the observed mean as prediction.

ndcg_at_k.description: Discounted cumulative gain over top-k normalized by the ideal ranking.

aliases: how much a clean result lowers the chance something is really there
when the test says no, how much does that shift the odds
how much more often clear cases get a negative than real cases do
the drop in suspicion you earn from a negative result
how convincing a no-finding actually is
by what factor a negative reading rules things out
negative_likelihood_ratio.description: Odds of a negative test result in cases versus non-cases.

aliases: of the ones it cleared, how many were truly clear
when it says no, how often is it right
if it comes back negative, what are the odds it's really fine
share of the all-clear calls that were actually all clear
how much you can trust a not-flagged verdict
negative_predictive_value.description: Fraction of negative predictions that are actually negative.

nelson_aalen_cumhaz.description: Nonparametric estimate of cumulative hazard over time.

net_debt_to_ebitda.description: Leverage using debt net of cash over operating earnings.

net_exposure.description: Long exposure minus short exposure relative to capital.

net_margin.description: Net income as a share of revenue.

normalized_hhi.description: Herfindahl index rescaled to 0-1 independent of the number of firms.

aliases: how much two groupings agree, on a clean zero-to-one scale
shared information between two labelings put on a comparable scale
agreement between two ways of splitting things, rescaled so it's fair to compare
how aligned two clusterings are regardless of how many groups each has
do these two partitions match, scored from none to perfect
overlap of information between groupings adjusted so size doesn't skew it
normalized_mutual_info.description: Mutual information scaled to a zero-to-one clustering agreement score.

npv.description: Present value of cash flows minus the initial investment.

nrmse.description: Root mean squared error normalized by the target range or mean.

null_fraction.description: Fraction of cells in a column that are empty or missing.

number_needed_to_treat.description: Number of patients treated to prevent one additional adverse outcome.

odds_ratio.description: Ratio of the odds of an outcome between two groups.

omega_ratio.description: Probability-weighted gains above a threshold divided by losses below it.

omega_sharpe_ratio.description: Omega ratio minus one; expresses omega as an excess performance figure.

operating_margin.description: Operating income as a share of revenue.

outlier_count.description: Number of values falling outside the Tukey IQR fences.

aliases: how likely is this result just from random chance
the odds of seeing this difference if nothing was really going on

chance of a fluke this big under no real effect

probability the observed effect is just randomness
how surprising this outcome would be if there were no true difference
p_value.description: Probability of results this extreme if the null hypothesis holds.

pain_index.description: Average depth of drawdowns over the whole period.

par_yield.description: Coupon rate that prices a bond exactly at par.

parametric_es.description: Expected shortfall computed assuming normally distributed returns.

parametric_var.description: Value-at-risk computed assuming normally distributed returns.

parkinson_volatility.description: Range-based volatility estimator using high and low prices.

partial_autocorrelation.description: Correlation of a series with a lag after removing intermediate lags.

participation_rate.description: Order volume as a fraction of total market volume over the trading window.

pass_at_k.description: Probability that at least one of k sampled solutions is correct.

aliases: is it bunched on one side with a long tail
gap between the typical and the middle value

percentage_agreement.description: Fraction of items two raters labeled identically.

percentile.description: Value below which a given percentage of the data falls.

aliases: how the top compares to the bottom

percentile_ratio.description: Ratio between two percentiles, summarizing spread between distribution tails.

portfolio_turnover.description: Fraction of the portfolio traded between two periods.

aliases: how much a flag raises the chance something is really there

pr_auc.description: Area under the precision-recall curve, useful for rare positives.

precision_at_k.description: Fraction of the top-k retrieved items that are relevant, averaged over queries.

predictive_parity_difference.description: Difference in positive predictive value across protected groups.

aliases: sum of gains over sum of losses
profit_factor.description: Gross profits divided by gross losses.

proportion_z.description: Tests whether two groups differ in their proportions.

psi.description: Population stability index measuring distribution shift between expected and actual.

purity.description: Fraction of items in the dominant true class of their cluster.

quartile_coefficient_dispersion.description: Quartile spread divided by quartile sum, a robust relative dispersion.

quick_ratio.description: Liquidity excluding inventory from current assets over current liabilities.

r2.description: Fraction of target variance explained by the model.

how clean the top slice is when sized to the number of true matches
share of the front results that were actually on target
accuracy of the head of the list cut at the count of right answers
r_precision.description: Precision measured at a cutoff equal to the number of relevant items.

r_squared.description: Fraction of return variance explained by the benchmark.

rachev_ratio.description: Expected tail gain divided by expected tail loss at chosen quantiles.

rand_index.description: Fraction of point pairs clustered consistently between two labelings.

aliases: gap between the biggest and smallest
difference between the top and bottom value
the full span of the numbers
range_value.description: Difference between the maximum and minimum values.

aliases: how big the difference between two groups is using ranks
strength of the gap between groups based on ordering not raw values
effect size from a rank-based comparison
how separated the two groups are when you rank everything
magnitude of the difference without assuming a bell curve

how meaningful the two-group difference is on a ranked scale
rank_biserial.description: Effect size for Mann-Whitney based on rank dominance.

ratio_share.description: Fraction of rows where a flag or condition is true.

rbp.description: Rank-biased precision weighting relevance by a geometric user-persistence factor.

realization_ratio.description: Share of fund value already returned as cash distributions.

realized_shortfall.description: Average of actually observed losses that breached VaR.

relative_mean_deviation.description: Mean absolute deviation expressed relative to the mean.

aliases: average pairwise gap sized against the typical

how much things differ scaled to their size

relative_risk_reduction.description: Proportional reduction in risk from treatment relative to control.

relative_spread.description: Bid-ask spread expressed as a fraction of the midpoint price.

relative_squared_error.description: Total squared error relative to a naive mean predictor's error.

rescaled_range.description: Range of cumulative deviations divided by standard deviation; basis of the Hurst exponent.

restricted_mean_survival_time.description: Average event-free time up to a specified time horizon.

roll_spread.description: Implied bid-ask spread estimated from the autocovariance of price changes.

row_count.description: Total number of rows in the dataset.

runs_test.description: Tests whether a binary sequence is randomly ordered.

sem.description: Standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the mean.

shannon_diversity.description: Shannon diversity index measuring richness and evenness of categories.

sortino.description: Excess return per unit of downside deviation.

specificity.description: Fraction of actual negatives correctly identified.

spread_dv01.description: Dollar price change per one basis-point move in credit spread.

squared_hinge_loss.description: Squared margin loss penalizing margin violations more steeply.

srm_pvalue.description: Tests whether observed group allocation matches the expected split ratio.

standardized_mean_difference.description: Group mean difference scaled by a pooled standard deviation.

starr_ratio.description: Excess return divided by conditional value-at-risk (expected shortfall).

sterling_ratio.description: Annualized return divided by average drawdown (often adjusted by a constant).

aliases: spread of the extremes in units of scatter
how far apart top and bottom are versus the noise

how stretched the ends are relative to noise

success_at_k.description: Whether at least one relevant item appears in the top-k results.

aliases: how much slower the worst cases are than the typical one
the gap between the slow outliers and the middle

stretch between the bad tail and the usual speed

how lopsided the slow end is compared to the median
tail_latency_ratio.description: Ratio of tail latency to median, measuring response time skew.

tail_ratio.description: Magnitude of the right tail relative to the left tail of returns.

test_coverage.description: Fraction of code lines executed by the test suite.

theil_index.description: Entropy-based inequality measure that decomposes across subgroups.

theil_sen_slope.description: Robust trend estimate from the median of all pairwise slopes.

theil_u1.description: Bounded forecast accuracy index between zero and one.

theil_u2.description: Forecast accuracy relative to a naive no-change forecast.

threat_score.description: Intersection over union of predicted and actual positives.

throughput.description: Number of operations completed per unit of time.

time_underwater.description: Fraction of time the equity curve spent below a prior peak.

aliases: how much the two answers overlap in the words they share

token_dice.description: Dice overlap coefficient between predicted and reference token sets.

partial credit for getting some of the right words even if not all
how well the answer's words line up with the expected words both ways

word-level closeness between a reply and the gold answer
score for sharing words without being exactly equal
token_f1.description: F1 score over the overlap of predicted and reference tokens.

token_jaccard.description: Jaccard similarity between predicted and reference token sets.

how often the correct one shows up among the top handful

how often it gets it right within its best few tries
chance the correct item appears in the shortlist
top_k_accuracy.description: Fraction of queries whose correct item appears within the top-k results.

total_return.description: Cumulative compounded return over the full period.

total_variation_distance.description: Maximum difference in probability assigned by two distributions.

aliases: how much we diverge from index
tracking_error.description: Volatility of return differences versus the benchmark.

tracking_signal.description: Cumulative error divided by mean absolute deviation to detect drift.

treynor_ratio.description: Excess return per unit of market beta.

trimean.description: Weighted average of the median and the two quartiles, a robust center.

trimmed_std.description: Standard deviation computed after removing extreme tail values.

tvpi.description: Total fund value per dollar of contributed capital.

ulcer_index.description: Root-mean-square of drawdowns; depth-and-duration measure of downside pain.

unexpected_loss.description: Volatility of credit losses around the expected loss.

uniqueness_ratio.description: Fraction of rows whose values are distinct, distinct count over total.

aliases: how much we gain in up markets
up_capture_ratio.description: Portfolio return relative to benchmark during the benchmark's up periods.

aliases: up market to down market sensitivity
up_down_beta_ratio.description: Bull-market beta divided by bear-market beta; beta asymmetry.

upside_deviation.description: Volatility computed only from returns above a target.

upside_potential.description: Average excess of returns above a target threshold.

uptime_pct.description: Percentage of time the system was operational.

aliases: how well the groups are both pure and complete at once

v_measure_score.description: Harmonic mean of homogeneity and completeness for clustering quality.

value_at_risk.description: Loss threshold the portfolio is unlikely to exceed at a given confidence.

var_breach_rate.description: Fraction of periods where losses exceeded VaR.

variance.description: Average squared deviation of values from their mean.

variance_ratio.description: Ratio of long-horizon to scaled short-horizon variance; tests random walk / mean reversion.

variance_reduction_cuped.description: Fraction of outcome variance removed by CUPED covariate adjustment.

vasicek_conditional_pd.description: Stressed default probability under an adverse systemic factor.

vwap_slippage.description: Cost of execution measured against the period's volume-weighted average price.

wape.description: Total absolute error as a fraction of total actual volume.

wasserstein_1d.description: Minimal cost to transform one distribution into another in one dimension.

weighted_average_life.description: Principal-weighted average time until a bond's cash is repaid.

weighted_f1.description: Support-weighted average of per-class F1 scores.

weighted_lgd.description: Average loss-given-default weighted by exposure size.

weighted_mean.description: Average where each value contributes in proportion to its weight.

wer.description: Word-level edit distance between transcription and reference divided by reference length.

wilcoxon_signed_rank.description: Nonparametric test for differences between paired observations.

willmott_index.description: Index of agreement between predictions and observations from zero to one.

wilson_lower.description: Lower bound of the Wilson confidence interval for a proportion.

wilson_upper.description: Upper bound of the Wilson confidence interval for a proportion.

win_loss_ratio.description: Number of winning periods divided by losing periods.

win_rate.description: Fraction of periods with positive returns.

winkler_score.description: Interval score rewarding narrow intervals and penalizing missed actuals.

winsorized_mean.description: Mean after capping extreme values at chosen percentiles instead of dropping them.

aliases: scatter with the extremes pulled in
how much things vary once the wild ends are capped
variation measured after clipping the tails
spread that ignores the most extreme bits
wobble computed with outliers reined in
dispersion robust to a few crazy values
winsorized_std.description: Standard deviation after capping extreme values at chosen percentiles.

yang_zhang_volatility.description: Range-based volatility estimator combining overnight and intraday moves using OHLC.

yield_to_maturity.description: Single discount rate equating a bond's price to its cash flows.

youden_j.description: Sensitivity plus specificity minus one, summarizing overall discrimination.

z_spread.description: Constant spread over the zero curve that prices a bond.
